title: allium douglasii var. tolmiei
intro: Allium douglasii var. tolmiei, also known as Tolmie's onion, is a type of wild onion native to the western coast of North America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Tolmie's onion is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
